why: The client IP is taken verbatim from the attacker-controlled X-Forwarded-For header without validating that the request actually arrived through a trusted proxy, allowing a remote attacker to spoof a loopback address and bypass the local-only check in install_mcp_config.
note: detector flagged a security-relevant pattern not covered by the rule corpus

why: The local-IP guard in install_mcp_config relies on get_client_ip which trusts X-Forwarded-For, so a remote attacker can set that header to '127.0.0.1' and invoke the install endpoint from any network.
note: detector flagged a security-relevant pattern not covered by the rule corpus

why: The client IP is read from the untrusted X-Forwarded-For header without validating that the request passed through a trusted proxy, allowing an attacker to spoof a loopback address and bypass the local-only check in install_mcp_config.
note: detector flagged a security-relevant pattern not covered by the rule corpus

why: install_mcp_config relies on get_client_ip which trusts X-Forwarded-For, meaning a remote attacker can forge a local IP in that header and bypass the is_local_ip guard that restricts config installation to local connections.
